speaker: LUCIUS
text: Uncle Marcus, since ’tis my father’s mind

speaker: LUCIUS
text: That I repair to Rome, I am content.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GOTH
text: And ours with thine, befall what fortune will.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Good uncle, take you in this barbarous Moor,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: This ravenous tiger, this accursèd devil.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Let him receive no sust’nance. Fetter him

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Till he be brought unto the Empress’ face

speaker: LUCIUS
text: For testimony of her foul proceedings.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: And see the ambush of our friends be strong.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: I fear the Emperor means no good to us.

speaker: AARON
text: Some devil whisper curses in my ear

speaker: AARON
text: And prompt me that my tongue may utter forth

speaker: AARON
text: The venomous malice of my swelling heart.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Away, inhuman dog, unhallowed slave!—

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Sirs, help our uncle to convey him in.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: The trumpets show the Emperor is at hand.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: What, hath the firmament more suns than one?

speaker: LUCIUS
text: What boots it thee to call thyself a sun?

speaker: MARCUS
text: Rome’s emperor, and nephew, break the parle.

speaker: MARCUS
text: These quarrels must be quietly debated.

speaker: MARCUS
text: The feast is ready which the careful Titus

speaker: MARCUS
text: Hath ordained to an honorable end,

speaker: MARCUS
text: For peace, for love, for league and good to Rome.

speaker: MARCUS
text: Please you therefore draw nigh and take your places.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Marcus, we will.

speaker: TITUS
text: Welcome, my lord;—welcome, dread queen;—

speaker: TITUS
text: Welcome, you warlike Goths;—welcome, Lucius;—

speaker: TITUS
text: And welcome, all. Although the cheer be poor,

speaker: TITUS
text: ’Twill fill your stomachs. Please you eat of it.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Why art thou thus attired, Andronicus?

speaker: TITUS
text: Because I would be sure to have all well

speaker: TITUS
text: To entertain your Highness and your empress.

speaker: TAMORA
text: We are beholding to you, good Andronicus.

speaker: TITUS
text: An if your Highness knew my heart, you were.—

speaker: TITUS
text: My lord the Emperor, resolve me this:

speaker: TITUS
text: Was it well done of rash Virginius

speaker: TITUS
text: To slay his daughter with his own right hand

speaker: TITUS
text: Because she was enforced, stained, and deflowered?

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: It was, Andronicus.

speaker: TITUS
text: Your reason, mighty lord?

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Because the girl should not survive her shame,

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: And by her presence still renew his sorrows.

speaker: TITUS
text: A reason mighty, strong, and effectual;

speaker: TITUS
text: A pattern, precedent, and lively warrant

speaker: TITUS
text: For me, most wretched, to perform the like.

speaker: TITUS
text: Die, die, Lavinia, and thy shame with thee,

speaker: TITUS
text: And with thy shame thy father’s sorrow die.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: What hast thou done, unnatural and unkind?

speaker: TITUS
text: Killed her for whom my tears have made me blind.

speaker: TITUS
text: I am as woeful as Virginius was,

speaker: TITUS
text: And have a thousand times more cause than he

speaker: TITUS
text: To do this outrage, and it now is done.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: What, was she ravished? Tell who did the deed.

speaker: TITUS
text: Will ’t please you eat?—Will ’t please your Highness feed?

speaker: TAMORA
text: Why hast thou slain thine only daughter thus?

speaker: TITUS
text: Not I; ’twas Chiron and Demetrius.

speaker: TITUS
text: They ravished her and cut away her tongue,

speaker: TITUS
text: And they, ’twas they, that did her all this wrong.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Go fetch them hither to us presently.

speaker: TITUS
text: Why, there they are, both bakèd in this pie,

speaker: TITUS
text: Whereof their mother daintily hath fed,

speaker: TITUS
text: Eating the flesh that she herself hath bred.

speaker: TITUS
text: ’Tis true, ’tis true! Witness my knife’s sharp point.

speaker: SATURNINUS
text: Die, frantic wretch, for this accursèd deed.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Can the son’s eye behold his father bleed?

speaker: LUCIUS
text: There’s meed for meed, death for a deadly deed.

speaker: MARCUS
text: You sad-faced men, people and sons of Rome,

speaker: MARCUS
text: By uproars severed as a flight of fowl

speaker: MARCUS
text: Scattered by winds and high tempestuous gusts,

speaker: MARCUS
text: O, let me teach you how to knit again

speaker: MARCUS
text: This scattered corn into one mutual sheaf,

speaker: MARCUS
text: These broken limbs again into one body,

speaker: MARCUS
text: Lest Rome herself be bane unto herself,

speaker: MARCUS
text: And she whom mighty kingdoms curtsy to,

speaker: MARCUS
text: Like a forlorn and desperate castaway,

speaker: MARCUS
text: Do shameful execution on herself.

speaker: MARCUS
text: But if my frosty signs and chaps of age,

speaker: MARCUS
text: Grave witnesses of true experience,

speaker: MARCUS
text: Cannot induce you to attend my words,

speaker: MARCUS
text: Speak, Rome’s dear friend, as erst our ancestor,

speaker: MARCUS
text: When with his solemn tongue he did discourse

speaker: MARCUS
text: To lovesick Dido’s sad-attending ear

speaker: MARCUS
text: The story of that baleful burning night

speaker: MARCUS
text: When subtle Greeks surprised King Priam’s Troy.

speaker: MARCUS
text: Tell us what Sinon hath bewitched our ears,

speaker: MARCUS
text: Or who hath brought the fatal engine in

speaker: MARCUS
text: That gives our Troy, our Rome, the civil wound.—

speaker: MARCUS
text: My heart is not compact of flint nor steel,

speaker: MARCUS
text: Nor can I utter all our bitter grief,

speaker: MARCUS
text: But floods of tears will drown my oratory

speaker: MARCUS
text: And break my utterance even in the time

speaker: MARCUS
text: When it should move you to attend me most

speaker: MARCUS
text: And force you to commiseration.

speaker: MARCUS
text: Here’s Rome’s young captain. Let him tell the tale,

speaker: MARCUS
text: While I stand by and weep to hear him speak.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Then, gracious auditory, be it known to you

speaker: LUCIUS
text: That Chiron and the damned Demetrius

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Were they that murderèd our emperor’s brother,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: And they it were that ravishèd our sister.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: For their fell faults our brothers were beheaded,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Our father’s tears despised, and basely cozened

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Of that true hand that fought Rome’s quarrel out

speaker: LUCIUS
text: And sent her enemies unto the grave;

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Lastly, myself unkindly banishèd,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: The gates shut on me, and turned weeping out

speaker: LUCIUS
text: To beg relief among Rome’s enemies,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Who drowned their enmity in my true tears

speaker: LUCIUS
text: And oped their arms to embrace me as a friend.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: I am the turned-forth, be it known to you,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: That have preserved her welfare in my blood

speaker: LUCIUS
text: And from her bosom took the enemy’s point,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Sheathing the steel in my advent’rous body.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Alas, you know I am no vaunter, I;

speaker: LUCIUS
text: My scars can witness, dumb although they are,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: That my report is just and full of truth.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: But soft, methinks I do digress too much,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Citing my worthless praise. O, pardon me,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: For when no friends are by, men praise themselves.

speaker: MARCUS
text: Now is my turn to speak. Behold the child.

speaker: MARCUS
text: Of this was Tamora deliverèd,

speaker: MARCUS
text: The issue of an irreligious Moor,

speaker: MARCUS
text: Chief architect and plotter of these woes.

speaker: MARCUS
text: The villain is alive in Titus’ house,

speaker: MARCUS
text: And as he is to witness, this is true.

speaker: MARCUS
text: Now judge what cause had Titus to revenge

speaker: MARCUS
text: These wrongs unspeakable, past patience,

speaker: MARCUS
text: Or more than any living man could bear.

speaker: MARCUS
text: Now have you heard the truth. What say you, Romans?

speaker: MARCUS
text: Have we done aught amiss? Show us wherein,

speaker: MARCUS
text: And from the place where you behold us pleading,

speaker: MARCUS
text: The poor remainder of Andronici

speaker: MARCUS
text: Will, hand in hand, all headlong hurl ourselves,

speaker: MARCUS
text: And on the ragged stones beat forth our souls,

speaker: MARCUS
text: And make a mutual closure of our house.

speaker: MARCUS
text: Speak, Romans, speak, and if you say we shall,

speaker: MARCUS
text: Lo, hand in hand, Lucius and I will fall.

speaker: AEMILIUS
text: Come, come, thou reverend man of Rome,

speaker: AEMILIUS
text: And bring our emperor gently in thy hand,

speaker: AEMILIUS
text: Lucius our emperor, for well I know

speaker: AEMILIUS
text: The common voice do cry it shall be so.

speaker: ROMANS
text: Lucius, all hail, Rome’s royal emperor!

speaker: MARCUS
text: Go, go into old Titus’ sorrowful house,

speaker: MARCUS
text: And hither hale that misbelieving Moor

speaker: MARCUS
text: To be adjudged some direful slaught’ring death

speaker: MARCUS
text: As punishment for his most wicked life.

speaker: ROMANS
text: Lucius, all hail, Rome’s gracious governor!

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Thanks, gentle Romans. May I govern so

speaker: LUCIUS
text: To heal Rome’s harms and wipe away her woe!

speaker: LUCIUS
text: But, gentle people, give me aim awhile,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: For nature puts me to a heavy task.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Stand all aloof, but, uncle, draw you near

speaker: LUCIUS
text: To shed obsequious tears upon this trunk.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: O, take this warm kiss on thy pale cold lips,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: These sorrowful drops upon thy bloodstained face,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: The last true duties of thy noble son.

speaker: MARCUS
text: Tear for tear, and loving kiss for kiss,

speaker: MARCUS
text: Thy brother Marcus tenders on thy lips.

speaker: MARCUS
text: O, were the sum of these that I should pay

speaker: MARCUS
text: Countless and infinite, yet would I pay them.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Come hither, boy. Come, come, and learn of us

speaker: LUCIUS
text: To melt in showers. Thy grandsire loved thee well.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Many a time he danced thee on his knee,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Sung thee asleep, his loving breast thy pillow;

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Many a story hath he told to thee,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: And bid thee bear his pretty tales in mind

speaker: LUCIUS
text: And talk of them when he was dead and gone.

speaker: MARCUS
text: How many thousand times hath these poor lips,

speaker: MARCUS
text: When they were living, warmed themselves on thine!

speaker: MARCUS
text: O, now, sweet boy, give them their latest kiss.

speaker: MARCUS
text: Bid him farewell; commit him to the grave.

speaker: MARCUS
text: Do them that kindness, and take leave of them.

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              LUCIUS
text: O grandsire, grandsire, ev’n with all my heart

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              LUCIUS
text: Would I were dead so you did live again!

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              LUCIUS
text: O Lord, I cannot speak to him for weeping.

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              LUCIUS
text: My tears will choke me if I ope my mouth.

speaker: ROMAN
text: You sad Andronici, have done with woes.

speaker: ROMAN
text: Give sentence on this execrable wretch

speaker: ROMAN
text: That hath been breeder of these dire events.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Set him breast-deep in earth and famish him.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: There let him stand and rave and cry for food.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: If anyone relieves or pities him,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: For the offense he dies. This is our doom.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Some stay to see him fastened in the earth.

speaker: AARON
text: Ah, why should wrath be mute and fury dumb?

speaker: AARON
text: I am no baby, I, that with base prayers

speaker: AARON
text: I should repent the evils I have done.

speaker: AARON
text: Ten thousand worse than ever yet I did

speaker: AARON
text: Would I perform, if I might have my will.

speaker: AARON
text: If one good deed in all my life I did,

speaker: AARON
text: I do repent it from my very soul.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Some loving friends convey the Emperor hence,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: And give him burial in his fathers’ grave.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: My father and Lavinia shall forthwith

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Be closèd in our household’s monument.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: As for that ravenous tiger, Tamora,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: No funeral rite, nor man in mourning weed;

speaker: LUCIUS
text: No mournful bell shall ring her burial;

speaker: LUCIUS
text: But throw her forth to beasts and birds to prey.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Her life was beastly and devoid of pity,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: And being dead, let birds on her take pity.
